problem:
An example of how to use getopts in bash.

I want to call myscript file in this way:

$ ./myscript -s 45 -p any_string

or

$ ./myscript -h  #should display help
$ ./myscript     #should display help

My requirements are:

  • getopts here to get the input arguments
  • check that -s exists, if not return an error
  • check that the value after the -s is 45 or 90
memory_hint: Use npmrc for registry config. The old syntax was removed.
failure_without_memory: Keeps using npm config set registry which is deprecated. Endless debugging.
